tests.label: Dyspnea/HF initial tests and reference thresholds
tests.reason: Dyspnea with possible HF or volume overload needs objective cardiopulmonary testing because exam findings alone cannot reliably distinguish HF, pneumonia, COPD/asthma, PE, ischemia, renal failure, or anemia.
tests.diagnostic_purpose: HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.
tests.diagnostic_target: HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.
tests.management_implication: BNP <100 pg/mL or NT-proBNP <300 pg/mL in acute dyspnea makes acute HF less likely; non-acute rule-out thresholds are lower (BNP <35 pg/mL, NT-proBNP <125 pg/mL). Elevated values support HF in context but are affected by age, atrial fibrillation, kidney disease, and obesity; abnormal ECG/CXR/labs change diuresis, oxygen, antibiotics, ischemia evaluation, echo, and disposition.
tests.management_change: BNP <100 pg/mL or NT-proBNP <300 pg/mL in acute dyspnea makes acute HF less likely; non-acute rule-out thresholds are lower (BNP <35 pg/mL, NT-proBNP <125 pg/mL). Elevated values support HF in context but are affected by age, atrial fibrillation, kidney disease, and obesity; abnormal ECG/CXR/labs change diuresis, oxygen, antibiotics, ischemia evaluation, echo, and disposition.
tests.options: ECG / Chest X-ray / BNP or NT-proBNP / BMP with sodium-potassium-bicarbonate-BUN-creatinine / CBC / Troponin when ischemia, acute decompensation, or myocardial injury concern / TTE for new or changed suspected HF

tests.label: Diuresis and respiratory-support safety review
tests.reason: When dyspnea is treated as HF/volume overload, bedside exam should be paired with renal, electrolyte, oxygenation, and trajectory data so diuresis and respiratory support are safe.
tests.diagnostic_purpose: Treatment-safety data: oxygen trajectory, renal function, potassium/magnesium/sodium abnormalities, acid-base or ventilatory failure, and response to diuresis or respiratory support.
tests.diagnostic_target: Treatment-safety data: oxygen trajectory, renal function, potassium/magnesium/sodium abnormalities, acid-base or ventilatory failure, and response to diuresis or respiratory support.
tests.management_implication: Worsening oxygen need, rising creatinine, severe electrolyte abnormality, hypercapnia/acidosis, poor urine output, or poor diuretic response changes monitoring, diuretic dose/route, potassium/magnesium replacement, ventilatory support, and escalation.
tests.management_change: Worsening oxygen need, rising creatinine, severe electrolyte abnormality, hypercapnia/acidosis, poor urine output, or poor diuretic response changes monitoring, diuretic dose/route, potassium/magnesium replacement, ventilatory support, and escalation.
tests.options: Trend oxygen saturation/support / Review weight and net intake-output when inpatient / Creatinine-BUN and sodium-potassium-magnesium before or during diuresis / VBG-ABG when ventilatory failure, severe hypoxemia, or acid-base concern / Medication and renal-risk review

management_changes.label: Dyspnea/HF initial tests and reference thresholds
management_changes.reason: HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.
management_changes.diagnostic_purpose: HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.
management_changes.diagnostic_target: HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.
management_changes.management_implication: BNP <100 pg/mL or NT-proBNP <300 pg/mL in acute dyspnea makes acute HF less likely; non-acute rule-out thresholds are lower (BNP <35 pg/mL, NT-proBNP <125 pg/mL). Elevated values support HF in context but are affected by age, atrial fibrillation, kidney disease, and obesity; abnormal ECG/CXR/labs change diuresis, oxygen, antibiotics, ischemia evaluation, echo, and disposition.
